root_text.sn46.3:1.1: “Ye te, bhikkhave, bhikkhū sīlasampannā samādhisampannā ñāṇasampannā vimuttisampannā vimuttiñāṇadassanasampannā, dassanampāhaṁ, bhikkhave, tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ bahukāraṁ vadāmi;
root_text.sn46.3:1.2: savanampāhaṁ, bhikkhave, tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ bahukāraṁ vadāmi;
root_text.sn46.3:1.3: upasaṅkamanampāhaṁ, bhikkhave, tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ bahukāraṁ vadāmi;
root_text.sn46.3:1.4: payirupāsanampāhaṁ, bhikkhave, tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ bahukāraṁ vadāmi;
root_text.sn46.3:1.5: anussatimpāhaṁ, bhikkhave, tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ bahukāraṁ vadāmi;
root_text.sn46.3:1.6: anupabbajjampāhaṁ, bhikkhave, tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ bahukāraṁ vadāmi.
root_text.sn46.3:1.8: Tathārūpānaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ dhammaṁ sutvā dvayena vūpakāsena vūpakaṭṭho viharati—kāyavūpakāsena ca cittavūpakāsena ca.
root_text.sn46.3:1.9: So tathā vūpakaṭṭho viharanto taṁ dhammaṁ anussarati anuvitakketi.
root_text.sn46.3:2.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tathā vūpakaṭṭho viharanto taṁ dhammaṁ anussarati anuvitakketi, satisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti;
root_text.sn46.3:2.3: satisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn46.3:2.4: So tathā sato viharanto taṁ dhammaṁ paññāya pavicinati pavicarati parivīmaṁsamāpajjati.
root_text.sn46.3:3.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tathā sato viharanto taṁ dhammaṁ paññāya pavicinati pavicarati parivīmaṁsamāpajjati, dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti;
root_text.sn46.3:3.3: dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn46.3:3.4: Tassa taṁ dhammaṁ paññāya pavicinato pavicarato parivīmaṁsamāpajjato āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ.
root_text.sn46.3:4.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno taṁ dhammaṁ paññāya pavicinato pavicarato parivīmaṁsamāpajjato āraddhaṁ hoti vīriyaṁ asallīnaṁ, vīriyasambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti; vīriyasambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti; vīriyasambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn46.3:5.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno āraddhavīriyassa uppajjati pīti nirāmisā, pītisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti; pītisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti; pītisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn46.3:6.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pītimanassa kāyopi passambhati cittampi passambhati, passaddhisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti; passaddhisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti; passaddhisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn46.3:7.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno passaddhakāyassa sukhino cittaṁ samādhiyati, samādhisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti; samādhisambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti; samādhisambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn46.3:7.2: So tathāsamāhitaṁ cittaṁ sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti.
root_text.sn46.3:8.1: Yasmiṁ samaye, bhikkhave, bhikkhu tathāsamāhitaṁ cittaṁ sādhukaṁ ajjhupekkhitā hoti, upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno āraddho hoti; upekkhāsambojjhaṅgaṁ tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhu bhāveti; upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo tasmiṁ samaye bhikkhuno bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchati.
root_text.sn46.3:9.1: Evaṁ bhāvitesu kho, bhikkhave, sattasu sambojjhaṅgesu evaṁ bahulīkatesu satta phalā sattānisaṁsā pāṭikaṅkhā.
root_text.sn46.3:9.4: No ce diṭṭheva dhamme paṭikacca aññaṁ ārādheti, atha maraṇakāle aññaṁ ārādheti.
root_text.sn46.3:9.5: No ce diṭṭheva dhamme paṭikacca aññaṁ ārādheti, no ce maraṇakāle aññaṁ ārādheti, atha pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā antarāparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.sn46.3:9.6: No ce diṭṭheva dhamme paṭikacca aññaṁ ārādheti, no ce maraṇakāle aññaṁ ārādheti, no ce pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā antarāparinibbāyī hoti, atha pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā upahaccaparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.sn46.3:9.7: No ce diṭṭheva dhamme paṭikacca aññaṁ ārādheti, no ce maraṇakāle aññaṁ ārādheti, no ce pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā antarāparinibbāyī hoti, no ce pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā upahaccaparinibbāyī hoti, atha pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.sn46.3:9.8: No ce diṭṭheva dhamme paṭikacca aññaṁ ārādheti, no ce maraṇakāle aññaṁ ārādheti, no ce pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā antarāparinibbāyī hoti, no ce pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā upahaccaparinibbāyī hoti, no ce pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti, atha pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti.
root_text.sn46.3:9.9: No ce diṭṭheva dhamme paṭikacca aññaṁ ārādheti, no ce maraṇakāle aññaṁ ārādheti, no ce pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā antarāparinibbāyī hoti, no ce pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā upahaccaparinibbāyī hoti, no ce pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā asaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti, no ce pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā sasaṅkhāraparinibbāyī hoti, atha pañcannaṁ orambhāgiyānaṁ saṁyojanānaṁ parikkhayā uddhaṁsoto hoti akaniṭṭhagāmī.
root_text.sn46.3:9.10: Evaṁ bhāvitesu kho, bhikkhave, sattasu bojjhaṅgesu evaṁ bahulīkatesu ime satta phalā sattānisaṁsā pāṭikaṅkhā”ti.

translation_text.sn46.3:1.1: “Mendicants, when a mendicant is accomplished in ethics, immersion, knowledge, freedom, or the knowledge and vision of freedom, even the sight of them is very helpful, I say.
translation_text.sn46.3:1.6: or go forth following them is very helpful, I say.
translation_text.sn46.3:1.8: Because after hearing the teaching of such mendicants, a mendicant will live withdrawn in both body and mind,
translation_text.sn46.3:1.9: as they recollect and think about that teaching.
translation_text.sn46.3:2.1: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of mindfulness;
translation_text.sn46.3:2.4: As they live mindfully in this way they investigate, explore, and inquire into that teaching with wisdom.
translation_text.sn46.3:3.1: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of investigation of principles;
translation_text.sn46.3:3.4: As they investigate principles with wisdom in this way their energy is roused up and unflagging.
translation_text.sn46.3:4.1: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of energy; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn46.3:4.2: When they’re energetic, rapture not of the flesh arises.
translation_text.sn46.3:5.1: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of rapture; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn46.3:5.2: When the mind is full of rapture, the body and mind become tranquil.
translation_text.sn46.3:6.1: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of tranquility; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn46.3:6.2: When the body is tranquil and one feels bliss, the mind becomes immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.sn46.3:7.1: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of immersion; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn46.3:7.2: They closely watch over that mind immersed in samādhi.
translation_text.sn46.3:8.1: At such a time, a mendicant has activated the awakening factor of equanimity; they develop it and perfect it.
translation_text.sn46.3:9.1: When the seven awakening factors are developed and cultivated in this way they can expect seven fruits and benefits.
translation_text.sn46.3:9.3: They attain enlightenment early on in this very life.
translation_text.sn46.3:9.4: If not, they attain enlightenment at the time of death.
translation_text.sn46.3:9.5: If not, with the ending of the five lower fetters, they’re extinguished between one life and the next.
translation_text.sn46.3:9.6: If not, with the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished upon landing.
translation_text.sn46.3:9.7: If not, with the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished without extra effort.
translation_text.sn46.3:9.8: If not, with the ending of the five lower fetters they’re extinguished with extra effort.
translation_text.sn46.3:9.9: If not, with the ending of the five lower fetters they head upstream, going to the Akaniṭṭha realm.
translation_text.sn46.3:9.10: When the seven awakening factors are developed and cultivated in this way these are the seven fruits and benefits they can expect.”
